oracle EXISTS: CochainComplex, Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.HomologicalComplex. Mathlib has no generic `cohomology` functor/decl at all (grepped Algebra/Homology + CategoryTheory: zero hits) -- only unrelated concrete instances (groupCohomology, localCohomology, sheaf cohomology, CohomologyClass) that are different, more specific objects. note: Point is not applicable here (see Point_geometry); for Minor: a minor is the determinant of a submatrix (a scalar), not the submatrix-selection operation itself. Mathlib has no dedicated `minor` def — only implicit uses of `(A.submatrix ...).det` inside Laplace-expansion lemmas (Matrix.det_succ_row, Matrix.det_succ_column, etc). `Matrix.submatrix` alone is matrix-valued, a different (weaker) object that merely shares a role.

verify_note: KILLED DiscreteTiling.PlacedTile. Oracle confirms it exists, but reading Mathlib.Combinatorics.Tiling.Tile.lean shows it is one of three building-block structures (Prototile, Protoset, PlacedTile); PlacedTile is a single placed copy of one tile (`index : ιₚ`, `groupElts : G ⧸ ...`) — a component used to build a tiling, not a tiling/tessellation itself. The module docstring explicitly frames these as pieces for a tiling to be built from, with no Tiling/IsTiling/covers-the-plane structure yet defined anywhere in the Tiling directory. Calling a single tile placement an 'exact' formalization of tessellation (a covering pattern of the whole plane) is a component-vs-whole faithfulness failure, not even a fair special_case. Reverting to not_formalized.

Mathlib's IsSaddlePointOn (Mathlib.Order.SaddlePoint, docstring cites Hiriart-Urruty (4.1.4)) is the DISTINCT game-theory/minimax saddle point of a two-argument function over a product domain X x Y: 'f a y <= f x b for all x in X, y in Y' — used for convex-optimization/Lagrangian duality, no gradient/Hessian involved. Same English name, different formal object; classic false-positive. Confirmed only one 'saddle' file exists in all of Mathlib and it is this order-theoretic minimax one, not the calculus stationary-point notion.
verify_note: Killed the sole formalization: name-collision between the calculus saddle point (this article) and Mathlib's distinct game-theoretic minimax saddle point. Status formalized -> not_formalized.

oracle EXISTS, module confirmed. CRITICAL FAITHFULNESS ISSUE FOUND: Summable's default summation filter is `unconditional`, and Mathlib proves `summable_abs_iff : Summable |f| ↔ Summable f` (Topology/Algebra/InfiniteSum/Order.lean:319) — i.e. for real-valued series, Summable f is equivalent to ABSOLUTE convergence, not mere (conditional) convergence. Mathlib's own module docstring explicitly warns: 'there are conditionally-summable sequences which are not unconditionally summable' (e.g. the alternating harmonic series, which is Wikipedia-convergent but NOT Summable). So Summable is a strictly NARROWER notion than Wikipedia's general 'convergent series' concept. Downgraded exact -> special_case (Summable captures only the unconditionally/absolutely-convergent case) and confidence high -> medium.

verify_note: Downgraded match_kind exact->special_case and confidence high->medium. label: Harmonic map

formalizations.evidence: Oracle EXISTS (confirmed via mathlib_search.py decl). Read source: def HarmonicAt := (ContDiffAt ℝ 2 f x) ∧ (Δ f =ᶠ[𝓝 x] 0), for f : E → F where E is a finite-dim real inner product space and F is merely a normed space (flat codomain, not a Riemannian manifold). This is exactly the codomain-flat special case of a harmonic map (tension field vanishing) between Riemannian manifolds — faithful as a special_case, not an exact match to the general manifold-to-manifold definition.

note: General harmonic-map-between-manifolds definition (tension field = 0) not in Mathlib; only the flat-codomain special case (harmonic functions) exists.

formalizations.evidence: Verified EXISTS via oracle and read source: the decl's own docstring literally labels it '**Hoeffding inequality** for sub-Gaussian random variables', not Bernstein's inequality. Bound is P(sum X_i >= eps) <= exp(-eps^2/(2*n*c)) for iid-independent subgaussian X_i with uniform proxy-variance c — same family of concentration/tail inequalities as Bernstein's classical bounded-variance form, but a distinct named result. grep -rIn 'Bernstein' over Mathlib/Probability/ confirmed zero hits for the named inequality itself.

label: cofibration
verify_note: KILLED HomotopicalAlgebra.Cofibration. Decl exists but read the source (CategoryWithCofibrations.lean:76-79): `class Cofibration : Prop where mem : cofibrations C f` — this is a vacuous bookkeeping typeclass meaning only 'f belongs to whatever class C designates as its cofibrations' in an arbitrary abstract CategoryWithCofibrations. It carries none of the actual mathematical content (homotopy extension property / cellular inclusion). Checked instances (grep across Mathlib): only for SSet, Over S, CochainComplex.Plus, Cᵒᵖ — model-category-theoretic settings, no instance for TopCat/topological spaces at all. Wikidata Q1048949 itself is described as 'continuous mapping between topological spaces' (confirmed via wikidata.py by_slug), i.e. the classical topological cofibration, which this decl does not formalize even as a special case — it's a same-name different-object collision. Downgraded exact→killed, not a genuine match.

formalizations.evidence: decl EXISTS (oracle confirmed). Lemma docstring: "**Gibbs' inequality**: the Kullback-Leibler divergence is nonnegative." Module-level docstring additionally states explicitly: "That lemma is our version of Gibbs' inequality ('the Kullback-Leibler divergence is nonnegative')." Statement `0 ≤ ∫ x, llr μ ν x ∂μ + ν.real univ - μ.real univ` under `μ ≪ ν` is the nonnegativity-of-KL-divergence content (klDiv itself is ℝ≥0∞-valued so nonneg by type; this lemma proves the underlying real quantity is nonneg, which is the substantive content of Gibbs' inequality).

label: Lévy process

formalizations.evidence: Verified EXISTS via oracle. Read definition: `HasIndepIncrements (X : T → Ω → E) (P : Measure Ω) := ∀ n t, Monotone t → iIndepFun (increments of X at t) P`. This is exactly the independent-increments clause, one of three defining properties of a Lévy process (independent increments + stationary increments + càdlàg). Missing stationarity and càdlàg, so correctly partial/special_case not exact.
